synsets.definition: a state of acute pain

properties.relation: intense suffering can dull other sensations
suffering is deeply felt in body

synsets.usage_example: She endured great suffering after the accident.

synsets.definition: make angry

properties.relation: anger is high in force or degree
anger takes over thought and action

synsets.usage_example: His cruel words filled her with anger.

synsets.definition: a vague unpleasant emotion that is experienced in anticipation of some (usually ill-defined) misfortune
synsets.usage_example: She felt anxiety rising as the interview approached.

synsets.definition: the trait of lacking enthusiasm for or interest in things generally

properties.relation: emotional response is muted and lifeless

synsets.usage_example: His indifference to the outcome was striking.

synsets.definition: an overwhelming feeling of wonder or admiration
synsets.usage_example: He stood in awe of the vast mountain landscape.

synsets.definition: the property of having a harsh unpleasant taste
synsets.usage_example: The bitter taste of the medicine made her grimace.

synsets.definition: a state of extreme happiness
synsets.usage_example: She felt pure bliss holding her newborn child.

synsets.definition: the humane quality of understanding the suffering of others and wanting to do something about it
synsets.usage_example: She showed great compassion towards the homeless people.

synsets.definition: happiness with one's situation in life
synsets.usage_example: He found contentment in his simple life.

synsets.definition: a quality of spirit that enables you to face danger or pain without showing fear
synsets.usage_example: She summoned the courage to speak up against injustice.

synsets.definition: a feeling of extreme pleasure or satisfaction
synsets.usage_example: She felt a delight wash over her when she saw the surprise party.

synsets.definition: the feeling that everything is wrong and nothing will turn out well
synsets.usage_example: He fell into despair when all his efforts proved futile.

synsets.definition: strong feelings of dislike
synsets.usage_example: She felt disgust at the sight of the rotting food.

synsets.definition: be afraid or scared of; be frightened of
synsets.usage_example: She felt fear grip her heart as the storm approached.

synsets.definition: street names for methylenedioxymethamphetamine
synsets.usage_example: He took go at the festival and felt energised all night.

synsets.definition: a feeling of intense anger
synsets.usage_example: The madness of his anger drove him to act recklessly.

synsets.definition: a feeling of melancholy apprehension
synsets.usage_example: A palpable gloom descended on the room after the announcement.

synsets.definition: a feeling of thankfulness and appreciation
synsets.usage_example: She felt deep gratitude for their selfless support.

synsets.definition: something that causes great unhappiness
synsets.usage_example: His grief at losing his best friend was almost unbearable.

synsets.definition: expect and wish
synsets.usage_example: She held onto hope that the surgery would be successful.

synsets.definition: sudden mass fear and anxiety over anticipated events
synsets.usage_example: When the fire alarm sounded, panic spread through the crowd in seconds.

synsets.definition: any object of warm affection or devotion

properties.relation: creates sense of intimacy and nearness

synsets.usage_example: She was the love of his life, someone he cherished and devoted himself to completely.

synsets.definition: an unfortunate development
synsets.usage_example: It was a shame that the concert had to be cancelled at the last moment.

synsets.definition: behave violently, as if in state of a great anger
synsets.usage_example: He stormed out of the meeting, slamming the door behind him in fury.

synsets.definition: a state of elated bliss
synsets.usage_example: She was in ecstasy when she learned she had won the award.

synsets.definition: a feeling of deep regret (usually for some misdeed)
synsets.usage_example: She felt deep remorse for her betrayal and could barely look her friend in the eye.

synsets.definition: a feeling of deep and bitter anger and ill-will
synsets.usage_example: Years of unfair treatment had filled him with such bitterness that he could no longer enjoy friendships.

synsets.definition: a disposition free from stress or emotion
synsets.usage_example: In the quiet sanctuary of the monastery, he found peace from his troubled mind.

synsets.definition: a painful emotion resulting from an awareness of inadequacy or guilt
synsets.usage_example: Shame washed over him as he realized everyone had witnessed his embarrassing mistake.

synsets.definition: an emotion of great sadness associated with loss or bereavement
synsets.usage_example: The old man sat alone, his heart heavy with sorrow for the daughter he had lost.

synsets.definition: prolonged unfulfilled desire or need

properties.relation: longing is felt as a physical ache
longing creates an empty feeling inside
longing stays with you over time
longing is characterized by intense yearning
longing has a nostalgic, wistful quality
longing combines desire with the pain of unfulfillment
longing is unfulfilled desire by definition

synsets.usage_example: She gazed out the window with a longing that no letter could satisfy.
